commit.message:
Fix Valuation sector filter + daily cache for market data

- Rewrite filter to use pre-computed inclusion set (isin) instead of
  map().isin() — eliminates silent NaN fallthrough that caused all
  companies to appear regardless of sector selection
- Reclassify Jio Financial Services and Sammaan Capital → "Other"
  so they no longer appear under Consumer Finance filter
- Replace always-live Yahoo Finance fetch with 24-hour disk cache:
  serve cached data when fresh, only refetch when stale or missing
- Update notes from "Refreshes every hour" → "Refreshes once a day"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: remove .venv-coach-gemma from tracking and add to .gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix APNs: remove content-available from alert pushes, add diagnostic logging

Bug: content-available:1 was combined with apns-push-type:alert, which Apple
docs explicitly say not to do — "This key shouldn't be combined with the
apns-push-type value of alert." This caused iOS to throttle/misclassify the
notification when the app was backgrounded or screen locked, explaining why
pushes only worked when the app was in the foreground (willPresent).

Changes:
- Removed content-available:1 from alert push payload
- Added badge:1 to aps payload
- Added full diagnostic logging: config values, bundle_id, host URL, APNs
  response status + reason (not just status code), apns-id on success

iOS entitlements confirmed correct: aps-environment=development and
UIBackgroundModes=remote-notification already present — no iOS changes needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

style: 달력과 Quick Actions 카드에 검은색 테두리 추가

- Tag picker: debounced search with & multi-term filtering

- Fix learn card done count: revlog type 0+2 now correctly feeds learning_done
- Fix new card done count: derived from first-ever revlog entries today (not stale scheduler.newCount)
- Remaining counts revert to fresh SQL queries instead of stale scheduler state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Valuation sector filter with explicit TICKER_SECTOR map

DB company names don't reliably match TICKER_MAP keys so the previous
sector lookup returned NaN for most companies. Add TICKER_SECTOR dict
keyed by exact TICKER_MAP names with correct sector per company, and
use it directly for filtering — guaranteed to match every ticker.

commit.message:
Fix register-device: accept token when ios_token missing/mismatched in users.json

Root cause: Render's ephemeral filesystem wipes users.json on restart, erasing
ios_tokens minted at login. The auth check (stored_token != provided_token) was
always failing for mark@train4life.life since his record has no ios_token.

Fix:
- If user exists but ios_token is missing → accept registration, save the token
- If user exists but ios_token mismatches → update to the app's token and save
- Log every field received and the stored token for comparison in Render logs

Also confirmed: mark@train4life.life has no apns_device_token in local users.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

운영 시간 제한 — KST 10시~14시만 watch 모드 실행

- railway.toml: cronSchedule '0 1 * * *' (UTC 1시 = KST 10시)

commit.message:
Remove redundant raw value labels from hbar charts

make_hbar computed raw text_labels (e.g. '423736.0') for annotations,
then callers also called update_traces(text=bar_label) adding a second
formatted label. Fix: add text_labels param to make_hbar so callers
pass formatted labels directly; remove duplicate update_traces calls.

Growth tab charts unaffected (they never had the duplicate labels).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make /pprof/symbol endpoint compatible with Go pprof symbolz protocol (#40)

Go pprof sends addresses separated by '+' and expects tab-separated
responses (0xaddr\tsymbol_name), but our endpoint only handled
newline-separated input and returned space-separated output. This caused
remote symbolization to fail when running pprof on Windows against a WSL
profiler server.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Deep Dive Revenues & PAT hover to show value not period

Two bugs: (1) loop checked 'NII' but trace name is 'Revenues' so
Revenues hover was never re-applied; (2) direct hovertemplate
assignment is unreliable — use trace.update() instead.
Now applies to all traces by name automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(harness-tests): v3 stripped results — two new failure modes

1. Sonnet removed its own reflectOn during reflection, permanently
   disabling self-modification. Ran 190 "look" ticks after.
2. Leftover admissible_commands in default on_tick caused both models
   to fixate on a field Jericho never populates.

Fix applied: removed admissible_commands from default entirely.
Chassis still doesn't protect against self-removal of reflectOn —
actant can trap itself. This is a real research finding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: phase 1 m1 — config, db schema, collector framework

First slice of the ArtimesOne foundation: scaffold the package, load
config with zero-env-var graceful degradation, lay down the full v1
SQLite schema (single migration 0001 — frozen across phases), and
define the Collector Protocol the scheduler will dispatch through.

Notable design choice: items_fts is a standalone FTS5 table rather
than the external-content variant the plan sketched. External-content
mode would require items.summary to exist as a column, but summary
text lives in md files — the plan flagged this trade-off and standalone
keeps the schema frozen in 0001 across phases.

ON DELETE CASCADE applied to all source/item/tag FKs up front so
Milestone 3's source-delete route doesn't need a schema bump.

commit.message:
Fix streaming: use ResponseHeadersRead so tokens appear live

PostAsync defaulted to ResponseContentRead which buffers the entire
response before returning — making streaming appear as a 22-second
freeze followed by a dump of all tokens at once. Fixed by passing
HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead so the body streams as the
model generates. Moved the Stopwatch to before PostAsync so elapsed
time now reflects actual inference time, not just buffer drain.

Also: installer now prints "source ~/.zshrc" hint when PATH was
updated so the user knows why 'matrix' isn't found immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add document vault for attaching single docs to chats

Adds a Document Vault feature that mirrors Knowledge Packs but stores
individual documents instead of bundles. Each entry has a name,
description, and a single file. Users can create, edit, and delete
vault documents from a sidebar modal, and attach any one of them to
a conversation via the + picker — the active document shows as a
chip above the input and as a pill on bot replies, exactly like
knowledge packs.

commit.message:
Rewrite as Memori AI platform with Stripe subscriptions, Capacitor Android, and WCAG AA

Landing page:
- Full Memori AI rebrand (AI-powered personal memory capture platform)
- Hormozi Value Equation hero: dream outcome H1, quick-answer block, proof badges,
  time/effort reducers (60-second setup, zero manual logging), 44px+ CTAs
- Grand Slam Offer section: bonus stack, price anchor vs. alternatives, 30-day guarantee
- TJ Robertson GEO rules: no accordions, self-contained paragraphs, question H2/H3s,
  fact-dense copy with specific numbers, freshness signal (April 2026), knowsAbout schema
- Comparison table: Memori AI vs Google Photos vs Apple Photos
- Social proof with 4.9/5 star reviews from 2,847 ratings

SEO/Schema (layout.tsx):
- Full JSON-LD stack: Organization, SoftwareApplication, WebSite, WebPage, FAQPage
- All entities cross-referenced via @id
- BreadcrumbList on /features, /pricing, /about, /blog
- Stable @id at https://memori-ai.app/#organization
- dateModified: 2026-04-01 freshness signal

WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast (tailwind.config.ts):
- Preserved backward-compat color names (accent, surface, text.*) for dashboard
- All landing page text uses gray-700+/violet-600+ on white (15:1+ ratio)
- Documented contrast values in comments; flagged failing combinations to avoid

Mobile-first design:
- clamp() font sizes, 44px min touch targets on all buttons/links/inputs
- Hamburger nav below md breakpoint with full mobile menu
- overflow-x-hidden on root, no horizontal scroll at 320px
- viewport meta tag present

New pages (stub + content):
- /features: 10 features with full prose descriptions, BreadcrumbList schema
- /pricing: Hormozi Grand Slam Offer + real Stripe checkout buttons, value stack
- /about: founder story, privacy commitment, values
- /blog: 5 article stubs with SEO-optimized titles

Stripe subscriptions:
- scripts/setup-stripe.ts: idempotent product/price creation (Starter $9, Pro $19, Family $29)
  with auto-update of .env.local price IDs
- src/app/api/checkout/route.ts: POST {priceId} → Stripe Checkout Session with 14-day trial
- src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts: handles checkout.session.completed,
  subscription.created/updated/deleted, invoice.payment_failed → upserts subscriptions table
- src/app/api/portal/route.ts: POST → Stripe Customer Portal session URL
- src/components/checkout-button.tsx: client component with loading state + error handling
- src/app/welcome/page.tsx: post-checkout success page with session confirmation
- supabase/migrations/20260409000000_create_subscriptions.sql: subscriptions table with RLS
- STRIPE_SETUP.md: complete setup guide (API keys, webhook config, portal setup, test cards)
- .env.example and .env.local updated with all required vars

Capacitor Android:
- capacitor.config.ts: app id com.coryslsbry.memoriai, remote URL mode for SSR compat
- PLAY_STORE_DEPLOYMENT.md: build commands, signing key generation, Play Console steps,
  permissions manifest, app icon sizes, CI/CD GitHub Actions pipeline, deep link config
- @capacitor/core, @capacitor/cli, @capacitor/android, @capacitor/splash-screen installed

next.config.mjs:
- Kept server-side rendering (no static export) to preserve API routes + Supabase auth
- Added images.remotePatterns for Supabase storage

commit.message:
security: full audit hardening — network exposure, XSS, credential leakage

- Bind port 8080 to 127.0.0.1 (was 0.0.0.0, exposed entire project to LAN)
- Restrict hl_config.json to chmod 600 (was world-readable)
- Remove tg_assistant_config.json from git tracking + gitignore
- Remove GitHub Pages origin from CORS allowlist
- Add security middleware: CSRF origin check + 1MB body size limit
- Cap SSE connections at 10 to prevent memory exhaustion
- Expand static file blocklist: block entire backend/ dir + .py/.sh/.db extensions
- Add AV + FRED API key proxy endpoints (keys no longer in browser URLs)
- Migrate OANDA + TickTrader credentials from localStorage to sessionStorage
- Remove window._TT_BOOTSTRAP global (race window, now redundant)
- Fix innerHTML XSS in addEventCard, logMsg, IC alert feed (hesc() escaping)
- Guard unguarded JSON.parse calls in reset modal
- Replace str(e) error responses with generic messages (stops internal leakage)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add # Errors and # Panics documentation to public methods (#393)

* Add # Errors and # Panics doc sections to public methods

Documents failure conditions on Result-returning methods and panic
conditions on methods that can panic. Focuses on non-obvious cases
in the app runtime, command, and worker modules.

- App::init(): adds formal # Panics section (default impl panics when
  called without override, previously only documented in # Default
  Implementation section which is non-standard)
- Command::save_state: adds # Errors section documenting the two
  distinct failure modes (silent no-op on serialization failure; error
  channel for file write failures), which is non-obvious behavior
- ProgressSender::send_percentage / send_status: adds # Errors sections
  consistent with the already-documented send() method

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

replace assets atomically. No more gap between delete and upload.

commit.message:
Fix num_ctx 400: include tool schemas in context calculation

Get-DynamicNumCtx was only counting message tokens, not tool schema
tokens. 15 tool schemas are ~1500+ tokens alone, pushing the prompt
over the 2048 floor and causing Ollama to return 400 Bad Request.

Fix: pass $Tools through to Get-DynamicNumCtx and include their
serialized JSON length in the token estimate. Raised minimum to 4096.
Also fixed keep_alive from string "-1" to integer -1.

Added a dedicated test suite for Get-DynamicNumCtx that would have
caught the bug: verifies num_ctx > tool token estimate, grows with
conversation, respects override, and aligns to 512 boundaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: matrix
repository.description: Matrix AI Agent — cross-platform hub (Mac, Linux, Windows)

commit.message:
fix(adapter): resolve session-ID capture for Gemini and Codex agents

StatusFileReader gated the events-file watcher on the Claude-only
statusFileHook, making hook-based session-ID capture dead code for
Gemini and Codex. Codex 0.118 also doesn't print the session ID in
PTY output or fire hooks. Both agents were stuck on "Loading agent..."
with no resume support.

- Decouple events.jsonl watcher from statusFileHook in StatusFileReader
  so raw lines reach captureHookSessionIds for all adapters
- Wire Gemini statusFile hook (GeminiStatusParser) for event parsing
  and hook-driven activity transitions (thinking/idle)
- Add fromFilesystem capture path for Codex: scans rollout JSONL files
  in ~/.codex/sessions/<today>/ matching by cwd + session_meta timestamp
- Fix Gemini locate() to match 8-char short prefix in chat filenames
- Add fromFilesystem to AdapterRuntimeStrategy.sessionId interface
- Include agent name in spawnAgent Command log line

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

react-spring animation patterns. Reference docs in .claude/references/.
README updated with usage instructions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: wisp
repository.description: A blazing-fast local component explorer for React + TypeScript.

commit.message:
fix: masses readiness — security, CI, billing, onboarding, and reliability

Security (critical):
- Remove OAuth HMAC truncation (was [:16], now full 64-char SHA-256)
- Instruction signing upgraded from plain SHA-256 to HMAC with secret key
- Require DATABASE_URL at server startup outside dev/test — prevents
  plan limits failing open and billing being bypassed
- WebSocket disconnect now signals engine abort, not just task.cancel()
- Playwright orphan process cleanup on server shutdown

CI:
- Fix all 226 ruff violations (36 auto-fixed, 7 manual E741 fixes)
- All checks now pass clean

Billing:
- Add customer.subscription.updated and customer.subscription.deleted
  Stripe webhook handlers — prevents plan downgrade/cancel bypass
- Add get_client_by_stripe_customer() DB helper for webhook lookups
- Make BYOK the free tier — new users start with unlimited executions
  using their own LLM key, no checkout required
- Update Clerk auto-provisioning to use "free" plan instead of "starter"
- Update portal Subscription page with Free (BYOK) tier

Portal:
- Wire History.jsx to real /history API endpoint (was empty DEMO_HISTORY)
- Add getHistory() to portal API client
- History shows loading/error states and real execution data

Extension:
- Add publisher field ("aerix") for VS Code Marketplace publishing
- Rename displayName from "Orion" to "AERIX"
- Update package name to "aerix"

Reliability:
- Increase DB connection pool from maxconn=10 to maxconn=20

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Ruff cleanup: remove unused imports and bare f-strings

- importers/baseball_cube_stub.py: remove unused csv and os imports
- fix_schema.py: remove spurious f-prefix on plain string literal
- importers/mlbstats_minor.py: same
- CLAUDE.md: refactored to lean coordinator pointing at vault files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

hide: 隐藏前端 stats 和状态筛选区域

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dynamic num_ctx from conversation size + timing logs

num_ctx is now computed per-request from actual message payload:
estimate input tokens (chars/3.5), double for response headroom,
round up to nearest 512. Short conversations get small KV caches;
long ones grow automatically. Config.NumCtx > 0 still pins a value.

Both chat functions now log elapsed ms, output token estimate, and
tok/s so performance is visible in err.log / matrix.log.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: matrix
repository.description: Matrix AI Agent — cross-platform hub (Mac, Linux, Windows)

commit.message:
Split chart/tests.rs to stay under 1000-line limit (#392)

* Split chart/tests.rs to stay under 1000-line limit

chart/tests.rs was at 1115 lines, the only file in the project
exceeding the 1000-line file size rule. Moves snapshot/rendering
tests into a separate file.
